   176→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   177→    base: "src/content/testimonials",
   178→  }),
   179→  schema: z.object({
   180→    quote: z.string(),
   181→    author: z.string(),
   182→    role: z.string(),
   183→    company: z.string(),
   184→    /** CDN path to author avatar (optional) */
   185→    avatar: z.string().optional(),
   186→    order: z.number().default(0),
   187→  }),
   188→});
   189→
   190→// ============================================================
   191→// Awards — recognition and validation
   192→// ============================================================
   193→// YAML entries for awards and recognitions. Shown on the homepage
   194→// in a scrollable list. Each award references the project it was for.
   195→
   196→const awards = defineCollection({
   197→  loader: contentLoader({
   198→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   199→    base: "src/content/awards",
   200→  }),
   201→  schema: z.object({
   202→    /** Award name (e.g. "Red Dot", "Awwwards Site of the Day") */
   203→    award: z.string(),
   204→    year: z.number(),
   205→    /** Project that won the award — validated at build time */
   206→    project: reference("projects"),
   207→    /** External link to the award page (optional) */
   208→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   209→    order: z.number().default(0),
   210→  }),
   211→});
   212→
   213→// ============================================================
   214→// Handbook groups — visual categories on the handbook index
   215→// ============================================================
   216→// Each group has a title and an order. Handbook pages reference
   217→// a group by its filename slug (e.g. "engineering", "design").
   218→
   219→const handbookGroups = defineCollection({
   220→  loader: contentLoader({
   221→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   222→    base: "src/content/handbook-groups",
   223→  }),
   224→  schema: z.object({
   225→    title: z.string(),
   226→    order: z.number(),
   227→  }),
   228→});
   229→
   230→// ============================================================
   231→// Handbook — internal company documentation
   232→// ============================================================
   233→// MDX files in src/content/handbook/, organized in directories
   234→// for parent-child nesting. File path determines URL:
   235→//   culture.mdx                      → /handbook/culture
   236→//   how-we-build-software.mdx        → /handbook/how-we-build-software
   237→//   how-we-build-software/front-end.mdx → /handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end
   238→//
   239→// `group` references a handbook-groups entry (validated at build time).
   240→// `order` controls sort position within a group on the index page.
   241→// Parent pages (those with children) are identified at query time
   242→// by checking if any other entry's id starts with their id + "/".
   243→
   244→const handbook = defineCollection({
   245→  loader: contentLoader({
   246→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   247→    base: "src/content/handbook",
   248→  }),
   249→  schema: z.object({
   250→    title: z.string(),
   251→    description: z.string().optional(),
   252→    /** Last updated date */
   253→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   254→    /** Cover image displayed at the top of the page (CDN path) */
   255→    coverImage: z.string().optional(),
   256→    /** Reference to a handbook-groups entry */
   257→    group: reference("handbook-groups"),
   258→    /** Sort order within the group on the index page */
   259→    order: z.number().default(0),
   260→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   261→  }),
   262→});
   263→
   264→export const collections = {
   265→  projects,
   266→  blog,
   267→  playground,
   268→  pages,
   269→  highlights,
   270→  clients,
   271→  testimonials,
   272→  awards,
   273→  "handbook-groups": handbookGroups,
   274→  handbook,
   275→};
   276→

1→/**
     2→ * Mosaic Icon System
     3→ *
     4→ * Icons are composed on a grid of cells (default 2px each). Each cell contains
     5→ * an "egg" (the Significa oval) at one of 5 scale levels. The size gradient
     6→ * across cells creates sub-pixel anti-aliasing.
     7→ *
     8→ * Icons can define two states (idle/hover) with different grid layouts.
     9→ * Transitions between states are driven by the parent via CSS custom properties:
    10→ *   --mosaic-state: 0 (idle, default) | 1 (active/hover)
    11→ *   --mosaic-duration: transition duration (default: --duration-fast)
    12→ *   --mosaic-ease: easing function (default: --ease-out)
    13→ *
    14→ * Color inherits via currentColor from the parent context.
    15→ */
    16→
    17→export type MosaicLevel = 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4;
    18→
    19→export interface MosaicIconDef {
    20→  /** Number of columns in the grid */
    21→  cols: number;
    22→  /** Default state grid (flat array of size levels) */
    23→  idle: MosaicLevel[];
    24→  /** Optional hover/active state grid */
    25→  hover?: MosaicLevel[];
    26→}
    27→
    28→/**
    29→ * Scale factor for each size level (fraction of cell size).
    30→ * 0 = invisible, 1(xs) = 27.5%, 2(s) = 57.5%, 3(m) = 87.5%, 4(l) = 100%
    31→ */
    32→export const MOSAIC_SCALES: readonly number[] = [0, 0.275, 0.575, 0.875, 1];
    33→
    34→/* prettier-ignore */

content.thinking: Now I have a complete picture. Let me write the Phase 2 audit report.

content.text:
---

# FASE 2 — Audit Report

## CLAUDE.md atual

**Existe?** Sim. 139 linhas. Cobre:
- Visão geral do projeto e stack
- Comandos de desenvolvimento
- 13 regras críticas (static-only, Astro-first, Content Collections, TypeScript strict, etc.)
- Estrutura do projeto (tree diagram)
- Convenções git (muito breve)
- Tabela de documentação com 9 docs de referência

**O que está em falta:**
- Zero menção ao sistema de design tokens (regras de uso, naming, o que nunca hardcodar)
- Zero menção ao sistema de mosaic icons
- Zero referência a fidelidade visual / processo de verificação contra Figma
- Nenhuma regra sobre font tokens (e o naming contra-intuitivo `--font-sans` = Google Sans Flex, `--font-display` = N27)
- Nenhuma secção sobre padrões de componentes (como se faz hover, estado, interatividade)
- Workflow rules ausentes (ler antes de escrever, verificar tokens, grep por hardcoded values)
- Quirks e decisões de arquitectura não documentados

**O que está desatualizado/errado:**
- A tree do projeto lista `labs/` em 4 sítios (linhas 69, 85-87) mas a collection real chama-se `playground/` e as pages estão em `src/pages/playground/`
- A tree não inclui `src/components/mosaic-icon.astro`, `src/lib/mosaic-icons.ts`, `award-row.astro`, `blog-card.astro`, `highlight-card.astro`, `project-card.astro`, `service-list.astro`
- A tree lista `src/components/ui/` como existente mas o diretório não existe
- Não menciona `handbook/` nem `handbook-groups/` nas collections

---

## Mosaic icon system

**Como funciona o hover:**
CSS custom property driven. O pai define `--mosaic-state: 0` (idle) ou `--mosaic-state: 1` (hover). O componente `mosaic-icon.astro` interpola entre os dois estados via `calc()`:

```css
--s: calc(var(--i) + (var(--h) - var(--i)) * var(--mosaic-state, 0));
```

Onde `--i` = escala idle da célula, `--h` = escala hover. Sem `:has()`, sem data attributes, sem JS. Puro CSS.

Na homepage (`index.astro:273-283`), o `.highlight-row` define `--mosaic-state: 0` por defeito e `--mosaic-state: 1` no `:hover`. O MosaicIcon filho reage automaticamente.

**Size prop:**
Não existe. O tamanho está hardcoded no CSS:
```css
width: calc(var(--cols) * 2px);  /* 7 cols × 2px = 14px */
```
Sempre 2px por célula. Para um icon de 7 colunas = 14px. Não há forma de mudar o tamanho sem override inline ou novo prop.

**Estrutura das definições em `mosaic-icons.ts`:**

```typescript
interface MosaicIconDef {
  cols: number;           // colunas da grid (ex: 7)
  idle: MosaicLevel[];    // flat array, length = cols × rows
  hover?: MosaicLevel[];  // opcional, mesmo tamanho
}
```

Cada célula é um `MosaicLevel`: `0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4`, mapeado para escalas de fração:
- `0` = invisível (mas renderiza como 0.275 via `max()` no CSS, dot muted a 20% opacity)
- `1` = 27.5% da célula
- `2` = 57.5%
- `3` = 87.5%
- `4` = 100%

A grid é row-major: os primeiros `cols` valores = primeira linha, etc. Rows = `idle.length / cols`.

**Formato de cada frame:** Flat array of MosaicLevel, lido left-to-right, top-to-bottom. O `arrow` icon é 7×7 = 49 células. O hover frame desloca o arrow ~1 célula para a direita/baixo, criando um efeito de movimento subtil.

Atualmente só existe 1 icon: `arrow`.

---

## Regras implícitas não documentadas

1. **Card pattern:** Todos os cards (highlight-card, blog-card, project-card) usam o mesmo padrão: `const Tag = href ? "a" : "div"` para renderizar como link ou bloco estático. Não está documentado.

2. **Palette tokens como props:** Os cards recebem cores de paleta raw (`var(--beige-50)`) como props `bg`/`fg` e passam-nas via CSS custom properties inline (`--card-bg`, `--card-fg`, `--media-bg`). Isto contorna a regra de "nunca usar palette tokens em componentes" porque o componente em si usa a custom property, não o token raw diretamente.

3. **Border hairlines:** O hero usa `border: 0.5px solid var(--color-border-subtle)`. O valor `0.5px` não tem token.

4. **Border-radius values:** `8px` e `0.75rem` usados em vários sítios sem token definido. Não há `--radius-*` tokens.

5. **`color-mix()` como técnica de opacidade:** `--color-border-subtle` usa `color-mix(in oklch, var(--color-text) 12%, transparent)`. Padrão não documentado.

6. **Mosaic icons herdam cor do pai:** Via `currentColor`. Não precisa de prop de cor.

7. **Collections helper layer:** Páginas nunca chamam `getCollection()` diretamente. Usam sempre `src/lib/collections.ts`. A excepção implícita é `getEntry()` que é chamado directamente em MDX components.

8. **Content loader strips extensions:** O `contentLoader` em `content.config.ts` remove extensões dos IDs para URLs limpas. Isto significa que `mishmash.mdx` tem ID `mishmash`, não `mishmash.mdx`.

---

## Inconsistências encontradas

### Valores hardcoded que violam o sistema de tokens

| Ficheiro | Linha | Valor | Deveria ser |
|---|---|---|---|
| `index.astro` | 213 | `max-width: 1512px` | Não existe token (valor legítimo de design, mas devia ser documentado) |
| `index.astro` | 332 | `padding: 200px 0 100px` | Valores px arbitrários |
| `index.astro` | 337 | `max-width: 500px` | Sem token |
| `index.astro` | 365 | `gap: 8px` | Padrão recorrente, sem token |
| `index.astro` | 378 | `padding: 16px` | Sem token |
| `index.astro` | 473 | `font-size: 0.75rem` | Deveria ser `var(--text-sm)` ou token |
| `header.astro` | 60 | `font-size: 0.625rem` | Sem token (logo dot) |
| `footer.astro` | 213 | `font-size: 0.625rem` | Sem token |
| `blog-card.astro` | 75 | `border-radius: 8px` | Sem token `--radius-*` |
| `highlight-card.astro` | 34 | `border-radius: 8px` | Sem token |

### Tokens referenciados mas não definidos

| Token | Usado em |
|---|---|
| `--text-xs` | `blog/index.astro`, `projects/index.astro`, `project-cross-sell.astro` |
| `--leading-relaxed` | `blog/[slug].astro` |

### labs/ vs playground/

O CLAUDE.md refere `labs/` em 4 sítios. A realidade do código é `playground/` em todo o lado:
- `src/content/playground/` (diretório)
- `src/pages/playground/` (diretório)
- `content.config.ts` define `playground` collection
- `collections.ts` tem `getPlayground()`

Os docs/ também têm referências a `labs`: `01-PROJECT-BOOTSTRAP.md`, `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md`, `05-RULES.md`, `07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md` (estes são documentação historica).

### Outras inconsistências

- `src/components/ui/` listado na tree do CLAUDE.md mas o diretório não existe
- Homepage usa dados hardcoded para 6 de 7 secções (awards, metrics, services, blog posts, highlight copy). Só highlights vem do content system.
- `showreel` section é um placeholder vazio com `min-height: 560px`
- `blog-card.astro` não suporta imagens (div colorido placeholder)

---

Fase 2 completa. Paro aqui e espero pela tua confirmação para avançar para a Fase 3.

message.content:
Confirmo. Avança para a Fase 3.

## Decisões tomadas

**Tokens a adicionar em tokens.css** (breaking — referenciados mas indefinidos):
- --text-xs → clamp(0.6875rem, 0.625rem + 0.2vw, 0.75rem)
- --leading-relaxed → 1.8
- --radius-sm → 0.5rem
- --radius-md → 0.75rem

Não tocas nos valores hardcoded existentes em index.astro — esses ficam para mais tarde.

---

## Fase 3 — O que fazer

### 1. Adiciona os 4 tokens em falta a tokens.css

Coloca-os na secção correta (typography scale para os dois primeiros, e cria uma secção "Border Radius" para os radius tokens se não existir).

---

### 2. Reescreve CLAUDE.md

Mantém tudo o que já existe e estava correto. Adiciona, corrige, ou substitui o seguinte:

**Corrige a tree do projeto:**
- Remove todas as referências a labs/ — substitui por playground/ onde aplicável
- Remove src/components/ui/ da tree — o diretório não existe
- Adiciona à tree os ficheiros que faltam: mosaic-icon.astro, mosaic-icons.ts, award-row.astro, blog-card.astro, highlight-card.astro, project-card.astro, service-list.astro
- Adiciona handbook/ e handbook-groups/ às collections

**Adiciona a secção: Design Fidelity Standard**

Todo o trabalho de UI tem de corresponder exatamente ao design de referência.

Antes de considerar um componente concluído:
1. Corre pnpm dev
2. Abre o browser e navega até ao componente
3. Tira screenshot
4. Compara contra a referência do Figma
5. Verifica cada propriedade:
   - Font family correto (--font-sans vs --font-display — ver nota abaixo)
   - Font weight correto
   - Tamanho de texto usa token da escala (--text-*)
   - Cor usa token semântico (--color-*)
   - Espaçamentos corretos (sem px arbitrários)
   - Bordas com 0.5px onde o design especifica
   - Border-radius usa token (--radius-sm, --radius-md)
   - Hover states transitam corretamente
   - Alinhamento correto
6. Se qualquer propriedade estiver errada — corrige antes de fazer commit.

O critério é: um designer a comparar os dois não consegue ver diferença.

**Adiciona a secção: Token System — Regras**

NUNCA hardcodes em ficheiros de componentes:
- Cores: sem hex, rgb(), oklch() literals
- Font families: sem strings "Google Sans Flex" ou "N27"
- Font sizes: sem px ou rem literals — usa var(--text-*)
- Espaçamentos: sem px arbitrários — usa rem em múltiplos de 0.25 ou tokens
- Transições: sem "150ms" literals — usa var(--duration-fast), var(--duration-normal)
- Border-radius: usa var(--radius-sm) ou var(--radius-md)

USA SEMPRE tokens semânticos (--color-*) em componentes. Nunca tokens de paleta raw (--gray-*, --beige-*, etc.) diretamente em componentes.

EXCEÇÃO DOCUMENTADA: Os card components (highlight-card, blog-card, project-card) recebem palette tokens como props de cor e consomem-nos via CSS custom properties internas (--card-bg, --card-fg). Este padrão é intencional e permitido — o componente em si usa a custom property, não o token raw.

ATENÇÃO — os nomes dos font tokens são contra-intuitivos:
- var(--font-sans) = Google Sans Flex → corpo, hero, UI geral
- var(--font-display) = N27 → labels, caps, metadados
Não trocar. Este naming existe por razões históricas e não vai mudar.

Se precisas de um valor que não existe como token:
1. Verifica se existe com outro nome
2. Se não existe, adiciona primeiro a tokens.css
3. Nunca um valor one-off diretamente num componente

**Adiciona a secção: Component Patterns**

Framework: Astro components (.astro). Sem React a menos que seja explicitamente necessário client-side.
Styling: blocos scoped dentro de .astro. Um componente, um ficheiro.
Estado e interatividade: CSS-only por defeito.

Hover states: pseudo-classe :hover
Estado driven pelo pai: CSS custom properties definidas no pai, lidas pelo filho
Classes condicionais: class:list={[{ 'is-active': condition }]}
Link vs bloco: const Tag = href ? "a" : "div" — padrão usado em todos os cards
JS/React: só se o CSS genuinamente não conseguir fazer o que é preciso

Props: sempre tipadas com interface Props {}. Sem any implícito.
Conteúdo: nunca hardcoded. Vem de props ou da content collection.

**Adiciona a secção: Mosaic Icon System**

Icons são grids 7×7 de elipses CSS renderizadas como "eggs".

Ficheiros:
- src/components/mosaic-icon.astro — renderer
- src/lib/mosaic-icons.ts — definições de icons

Formato de cada icon:
```ts
interface MosaicIconDef {
  cols: number           // normalmente 7
  idle: MosaicLevel[]   // flat array, length = cols × rows
  hover?: MosaicLevel[] // opcional, mesmo tamanho
}

type MosaicLevel = 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4
// 0 = invisível | 1 = 27.5% | 2 = 57.5% | 3 = 87.5% | 4 = 100% da célula
```

Array é row-major: primeiros `cols` valores = primeira linha, left-to-right, top-to-bottom.

Hover mechanism: CSS custom property no pai.
- Pai define --mosaic-state: 0 (idle) ou --mosaic-state: 1 (hover) via :hover
- O MosaicIcon interpola automaticamente via calc()
- Zero JS. Zero React. Zero data attributes.

Tamanho: atualmente hardcoded a 2px/célula (14px para grid 7×7).
TODO (Session 2): adicionar prop size para suportar 14, 16, 20, 24px.

Para adicionar um icon novo: seguir o padrão exato do arrow existente em mosaic-icons.ts.
Nunca criar SVG icons para elementos que pertencem ao sistema mosaic.

Cor: os icons herdam cor do pai via currentColor. Sem prop de cor.

**Adiciona a secção: Content & Data**

Todo o conteúdo vive em src/content/ como ficheiros MDX/YAML tipados.
Acesso via src/lib/collections.ts — nunca chamar getCollection() diretamente em pages.
Exceção documentada: getEntry() pode ser chamado diretamente em MDX components.
Schema validado por Zod em src/content.config.ts.
IDs de content não têm extensão (mishmash, não mishmash.mdx) — o loader remove-as.

**Adiciona a secção: Git Workflow**

Cada sessão de trabalho termina com commit. Sem exceções.

Após cada unidade de trabalho:
  git add -A
  git commit -m "<verb> <what>: <descrição breve>"

Formato: inglês, modo imperativo, específico.
- Correto: "add highlight-row component with CSS hover states"
- Errado: "update components", "fix stuff", "wip"

Uma mudança lógica por commit.

Branching:
  feature/<short-description>
  fix/<short-description>
  chore/<short-description>

**Adiciona a secção: Quirks & Decisões**

- Font token naming: --font-sans = Google Sans Flex (corpo), --font-display = N27 (labels). Naming herdado, não vai mudar.
- N27 servido como OTF (não WOFF2). Dívida de performance. ~210KB vs ~90KB estimado em WOFF2. Marcar para resolver.
- React instalado (@astrojs/react, react, react-dom) mas zero componentes React existem. Configurado para futuros islands se necessário.
- Homepage (index.astro) usa dados hardcoded em 6 de 7 secções. Só highlights vem do content system. Trabalho em curso.
- src/components/ui/ não existe apesar de referenciado em documentação antiga.
- 0.5px borders não têm token. É um valor de design intencional. Usar literal 0.5px é a exceção aceite.

**Adiciona a secção: Workflow Rules**

Antes de escrever código:
1. Lê os ficheiros relevantes existentes. Entende o padrão antes de adicionar.
2. Verifica se existe um token para o valor que precisas.
3. Verifica se já existe um componente que faz o que precisas.
4. Para tarefas com mais de um ficheiro: escreve o plano antes de executar.

Quando algo é ambíguo: para e pergunta. Não adivinhas. Não inventas.

Quando terminares qualquer componente:
- Tira screenshot, compara com referência de design
- Grep nos ficheiros modificados por valores hardcoded
- git diff antes de commitar — lê cada linha

---

### 3. Corrige as referências labs/ nos docs/

Nos ficheiros docs/01-PROJECT-BOOTSTRAP.md, docs/02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md, docs/05-RULES.md, docs/07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md:
- Substitui labs/ por playground/ onde o contexto se refere à collection/pages existente
- Se alguma referência for histórica e o contexto for claro, mantém com uma nota "(agora playground/)"

---

### 4. Cria .claude/settings.json
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(git add:*)",
      "Bash(git commit:*)",
      "Bash(git status:*)",
      "Bash(git diff:*)",
      "Bash(git log:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm install:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm dev:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm build:*)",
      "Bash(pnpm typecheck:*)"
    ]
  }
}
```

---

### 5. Commit
```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: audit and rewrite CLAUDE.md with token and design standards"
```

---

## Output

Mostra-me:
1. O conteúdo completo do CLAUDE.md final
2. O conteúdo do .claude/settings.json
3. O diff de tokens.css
4. Lista dos docs/ modificados
5. O hash do commit

file.content:
# Project Bootstrap

## Stack

- **Astro v5** (latest stable): static site generator, zero JS by default. Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs) — do not upgrade until stable.
- **MDX**: markdown with custom components for case studies and rich content
- **TypeScript**: strict mode, no `any`
- **Content Collections**: Astro's built-in content layer with Zod schemas
- **React**: only for interactive islands (mobile nav, form validation). Keep it minimal.
- **pnpm**: package manager
- **Cloudflare Pages**: static deploy target

## Init

```bash
pnpm create astro@latest significa-website -- --template minimal --typescript strict
cd significa-website
pnpm astro add mdx react
```

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── components/
│   ├── mdx/                  # Components available inside MDX files
│   │   ├── media-image.astro
│   │   ├── media-video.astro
│   │   ├── comparison-block.astro
│   │   ├── metrics.astro
│   │   ├── project-cross-sell.astro
│   │   └── components.ts      # Centralized MDX component registry
│   ├── ui/                   # Reusable UI components
│   ├── header.astro
│   ├── footer.astro
│   └── seo.astro             # SEO meta tags component
├── content.config.ts         # Collection schemas (the "database")
├── content/
│   ├── projects/             # .mdx per project case study
│   ├── blog/                 # .md or .mdx per blog post
│   ├── labs/                 # .mdx per open source project
│   ├── pages/                # .mdx per misc page (about, services, etc.)
│   ├── highlights/           # .yaml per homepage highlight
│   ├── clients/              # .yaml per client (logo strip)
│   ├── testimonials/         # .yaml per testimonial
│   └── awards/               # .yaml per award
├── layouts/
│   └── base.astro            # Main HTML layout with SEO component
├── pages/
│   ├── index.astro           # Homepage
│   ├── who-we-are.astro      # About page (hybrid: Astro page, not MDX)
│   ├── what-we-do.astro      # Services page (hybrid)
│   ├── work-with-us.astro    # Contact page (hybrid)
│   ├── blog/
│   │   ├── index.astro       # Blog listing
│   │   ├── [slug].astro      # Blog post detail
│   │   └── rss.xml.ts        # RSS feed
│   ├── projects/
│   │   ├── index.astro       # Projects listing
│   │   └── [slug].astro      # Project detail
│   ├── labs/
│   │   ├── index.astro       # Labs listing
│   │   └── [slug].astro      # Lab detail
│   ├── 404.astro             # Custom 404 page
│   └── [...slug].astro       # Catch-all for misc content pages
├── styles/
│   ├── global.css            # Entry point: imports reset + tokens, base styles
│   ├── reset.css             # CSS reset
│   └── tokens.css            # Design tokens (colors, typography, layout, transitions)
└── lib/
    ├── collections.ts        # Helper functions for content queries
    ├── cdn.ts                # CDN URL helpers and srcset generation
    ├── seo.ts                # Structured data generation (JSON-LD)
    └── content-errors.ts     # Build-time error formatting
public/
├── favicon.svg
├── fonts/
└── robots.txt
# Images/videos served via S3 + CDN (cdn.significa.co) (see 04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md)
```

### Hybrid page architecture

Some pages are hardcoded Astro files (`who-we-are.astro`, `what-we-do.astro`, `work-with-us.astro`) because they have complex layouts that don't fit the MDX pattern. Other pages (about, services, etc.) live as MDX in `src/content/pages/` and are rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.

The reserved routes list in `[...slug].astro` prevents collisions between the two approaches.

## Key Principles

1. **No database.** Content lives in MDX/YAML files with typed frontmatter. Content Collections are the database.
2. **No client-side JS by default.** Astro renders everything at build time. Only add `client:load` or `client:visible` to React components that genuinely need interactivity.
3. **Media via S3 + self-hosted CDN.** Images and videos are uploaded through the internal asset manager to S3 and served via `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare → Fly.io transform service → S3) with sharp-based image optimization. See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`.
4. **Relationships via `reference()`.** Collections reference each other using Astro's `reference()` for build-time validation. Broken slugs break the build, not production.
5. **Drafts via `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. The shared `contentLoader()` excludes `*.draft.*` files in production builds at the glob level. In development, drafts are included for preview. Publishing is a file rename — no frontmatter field needed.
6. **Keep it simple.** If you're reaching for a library, stop and check if plain HTML/CSS or an Astro component can do it.
7. **Fail at build time.** The site is managed by marketing and non-technical people. Every error caught at build time is one less bug in production.
8. **Centralized MDX registration.** All MDX components are registered in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`. Add new components there — all slug pages pick them up automatically.

## Build & Deploy

```bash
pnpm dev          # Development server with HMR
pnpm build        # Build static site to dist/
pnpm preview      # Preview built site locally
pnpm check        # TypeScript + content validation (astro check)
pnpm lint         # Full lint pipeline: Prettier + type check + ESLint
pnpm lint:check   # ESLint only
pnpm lint:fix     # ESLint auto-fix
pnpm format       # Prettier auto-format src/
pnpm format:check # Prettier check (no write)
```

### CI Pipeline

```bash
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm lint       # Prettier + type check + ESLint (catches all code quality issues)
pnpm build      # Full build (catches MDX errors, broken references)
```

### Deploy

Cloudflare Pages: push to main triggers build and deploy of `dist/`. Branch pushes create preview deployments.

### Pre-Push Checklist

Before pushing, run `pnpm lint && pnpm build` locally. This runs Prettier, type checking, and ESLint before building. Content errors caught locally are 10x faster than waiting for CI.

## Reference Documentation

- **Astro docs:** https://docs.astro.build
- **Content Collections:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/content-collections/
- **MDX in Astro:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/markdown-content/
- **View Transitions:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/view-transitions/
- **Islands Architecture:** https://docs.astro.build/en/concepts/islands/

**Do not hallucinate APIs.** If you're unsure whether a method or config option exists, check the docs. Astro's API surface is small.

content.content:
1→# Content Schema & Relationships
     2→
     3→Defines the "database": all content types, their fields, and how they relate to each other.
     4→
     5→## Schema Definition
     6→
     7→File: `src/content.config.ts` (at the root of `src/`, not inside `src/content/`)
     8→
     9→### Shared Schemas
    10→
    11→```ts
    12→import { defineCollection, reference, z } from "astro:content";
    13→import { glob, type Loader } from "astro/loaders";
    14→
    15→const seoSchema = z.object({
    16→  metaTitle: z.string().optional(),
    17→  metaDescription: z.string().optional(),
    18→  ogImage: z.string().optional(),
    19→});
    20→
    21→const metricSchema = z.object({
    22→  value: z.string(),
    23→  label: z.string(),
    24→});
    25→```
    26→
    27→### Content Loader
    28→
    29→All collections use a shared `contentLoader()` helper that centralizes glob patterns, ID generation, and **draft filtering**:
    30→
    31→```ts
    32→const contentLoader = ({ base, extensions }: { extensions: string[]; base: string }): Loader => {
    33→  const stripExtension = ({ entry }: { entry: string }) => {
    34→    return entry.replace(/\.(mdx?|yaml)$/, "");
    35→  };
    36→
    37→  // In development: include all files (drafts visible for preview)
    38→  // In production: exclude *.draft.* files at the glob level
    39→  const draftFilter = import.meta.env.MODE === "development" ? "**" : "!(*.draft)";
    40→  const extensionFilter = extensions.length > 1 ? `{${extensions.join(",")}}` : extensions[0];
    41→
    42→  return glob({
    43→    pattern: `**/${draftFilter}.${extensionFilter}`,
    44→    base,
    45→    generateId: stripExtension,
    46→  });
    47→};
    48→```
    49→
    50→This means:
    51→
    52→- **Development** (`pnpm dev`): all files are loaded, including `*.draft.*` files, so authors can preview drafts locally.
    53→- **Production** (`pnpm build`): files matching `*.draft.*` are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer.
    54→- **Publishing a draft** is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`.
    55→- **ID generation** strips file extensions for clean URL slugs: `example-project.mdx` → `example-project`.
    56→
    57→## Collections
    58→
    59→### Projects (MDX)
    60→
    61→Case studies. The richest content type.
    62→
    63→```ts
    64→const projects = defineCollection({
    65→  loader: contentLoader({
    66→    base: "src/content/projects",
    67→    extensions: ["mdx"],
    68→  }),
    69→  schema: z.object({
    70→    title: z.string(),
    71→    tagline: z.string().optional(),
    72→    client: z.string().optional(),
    73→    date: z.coerce.date(),
    74→    thumbnail: z.string(),
    75→    heroImage: z.string().optional(),
    76→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    77→    metrics: z.array(metricSchema).default([]),
    78→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
    79→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
    80→  }),
    81→});
    82→```
    83→
    84→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
    85→|---|---|---|---|
    86→| `title` | string | yes | Project name |
    87→| `tagline` | string | no | Short description for cards |
    88→| `client` | string | no | Client company name |
    89→| `date` | date | yes | Project date (used for sorting) |
    90→| `thumbnail` | string | yes | CDN path for listing cards |
    91→| `heroImage` | string | no | CDN path for showcase/detail header |
    92→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
    93→| `metrics` | metric[] | no | Key metrics shown on project cards (e.g. `{ value: "55%↑", label: "Conversion" }`) |
    94→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
    95→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Build-time validated slugs of related projects |
    96→
    97→### Blog (MD/MDX)
    98→
    99→Blog posts. Supports both `.md` (no components) and `.mdx` (with components).
   100→
   101→```ts
   102→const blog = defineCollection({
   103→  loader: contentLoader({
   104→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   105→    base: "src/content/blog",
   106→  }),
   107→  schema: z.object({
   108→    title: z.string(),
   109→    description: z.string().optional(),
   110→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   111→    author: z.string().optional(),
   112→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   113→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   114→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   115→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
   116→  }),
   117→});
   118→```
   119→
   120→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   121→|---|---|---|---|
   122→| `title` | string | yes | Post title |
   123→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and RSS. Falls back to title-based string if missing |
   124→| `date` | date | yes | Publication date |
   125→| `author` | string | no | Author name |
   126→| `thumbnail` | string | no | CDN path for listing cards |
   127→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
   128→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   129→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Cross-sell to project case studies |
   130→
   131→### Labs (MD/MDX)
   132→
   133→Open source projects and experiments.
   134→
   135→```ts
   136→const labs = defineCollection({
   137→  loader: contentLoader({
   138→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   139→    base: "src/content/labs",
   140→  }),
   141→  schema: z.object({
   142→    title: z.string(),
   143→    description: z.string(),
   144→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   145→    repoUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
   146→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   147→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   148→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   149→  }),
   150→});
   151→```
   152→
   153→### Pages (MDX)
   154→
   155→Miscellaneous content pages (about, services, etc.) rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.
   156→
   157→```ts
   158→const pages = defineCollection({
   159→  loader: contentLoader({
   160→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   161→    base: "src/content/pages",
   162→  }),
   163→  schema: z.object({
   164→    title: z.string(),
   165→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   166→  }),
   167→});
   168→```
   169→
   170→### Highlights (YAML)
   171→
   172→Homepage curation. Each entry references an item in another collection using a discriminated union. Astro validates every slug at build time.
   173→
   174→```ts
   175→const highlights = defineCollection({
   176→  loader: contentLoader({
   177→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   178→    base: "src/content/highlights",
   179→  }),
   180→  schema: z.object({
   181→    label: z.string().optional(),
   182→    source: z.discriminatedUnion("collection", [
   183→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("projects"), entry: reference("projects") }),
   184→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("blog"), entry: reference("blog") }),
   185→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("labs"), entry: reference("labs") }),
   186→    ]),
   187→    order: z.number(),
   188→  }),
   189→});
   190→```
   191→
   192→Example YAML:
   193→
   194→```yaml
   195→# src/content/highlights/01-cool-project.yaml
   196→order: 1
   197→label: Featured Project
   198→source:
   199→  collection: projects
   200→  entry: example-project
   201→```
   202→
   203→If `example-project` doesn't exist in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails** with a clear error. If the referenced file is a draft (e.g. `example-project.draft.mdx`), it works in dev but **fails the production build** — preventing links to unpublished content.
   204→
   205→### Clients (YAML)
   206→
   207→Client logo strip and references across the site.
   208→
   209→```ts
   210→const clients = defineCollection({
   211→  loader: contentLoader({
   212→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   213→    base: "src/content/clients",
   214→  }),
   215→  schema: z.object({
   216→    name: z.string(),
   217→    logo: z.string(),
   218→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   219→    order: z.number().default(0),
   220→  }),
   221→});
   222→```
   223→
   224→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   225→|---|---|---|---|
   226→| `name` | string | yes | Client company name |
   227→| `logo` | string | yes | Path to SVG logo in `public/` (e.g. `/logos/boeing.svg`) |
   228→| `url` | string | no | Client website URL |
   229→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   230→
   231→### Testimonials (YAML)
   232→
   233→Customer quotes for social proof sections.
   234→
   235→```ts
   236→const testimonials = defineCollection({
   237→  loader: contentLoader({
   238→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   239→    base: "src/content/testimonials",
   240→  }),
   241→  schema: z.object({
   242→    quote: z.string(),
   243→    author: z.string(),
   244→    role: z.string(),
   245→    company: z.string(),
   246→    avatar: z.string().optional(),
   247→    order: z.number().default(0),
   248→  }),
   249→});
   250→```
   251→
   252→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   253→|---|---|---|---|
   254→| `quote` | string | yes | The testimonial text |
   255→| `author` | string | yes | Person's name |
   256→| `role` | string | yes | Job title |
   257→| `company` | string | yes | Company name |
   258→| `avatar` | string | no | CDN path to author photo |
   259→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   260→
   261→### Awards (YAML)
   262→
   263→Recognition and validation. Each award references a project.
   264→
   265→```ts
   266→const awards = defineCollection({
   267→  loader: contentLoader({
   268→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   269→    base: "src/content/awards",
   270→  }),
   271→  schema: z.object({
   272→    award: z.string(),
   273→    year: z.number(),
   274→    project: reference("projects"),
   275→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   276→    order: z.number().default(0),
   277→  }),
   278→});
   279→```
   280→
   281→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   282→|---|---|---|---|
   283→| `award` | string | yes | Award name (e.g. "Red Dot", "Awwwards SOTD") |
   284→| `year` | number | yes | Year awarded |
   285→| `project` | reference | yes | Project that won — build-time validated |
   286→| `url` | string | no | External link to award page |
   287→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   288→
   289→### Handbook Groups (YAML)
   290→
   291→Visual categories shown on the handbook index page. Each handbook page references one group.
   292→
   293→```ts
   294→const handbookGroups = defineCollection({
   295→  loader: contentLoader({
   296→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   297→    base: "src/content/handbook-groups",
   298→  }),
   299→  schema: z.object({
   300→    title: z.string(),
   301→    order: z.number(),
   302→  }),
   303→});
   304→```
   305→
   306→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   307→|---|---|---|---|
   308→| `title` | string | yes | Display name shown on the index page |
   309→| `order` | number | yes | Sort order of the group on the index page |
   310→
   311→Filenames act as slugs referenced by handbook pages (e.g. `engineering.yaml` → `group: engineering`).
   312→
   313→### Handbook (MDX)
   314→
   315→Internal company documentation. File path determines both the URL and the parent-child hierarchy.
   316→
   317→```ts
   318→const handbook = defineCollection({
   319→  loader: contentLoader({
   320→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   321→    base: "src/content/handbook",
   322→  }),
   323→  schema: z.object({
   324→    title: z.string(),
   325→    description: z.string().optional(),
   326→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   327→    group: reference("handbook-groups"),
   328→    order: z.number().default(0),
   329→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   330→  }),
   331→});
   332→```
   333→
   334→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   335→|---|---|---|---|
   336→| `title` | string | yes | Page title |
   337→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and child page listings |
   338→| `date` | date | yes | Last updated date |
   339→| `group` | reference | yes | Which group this page belongs to — build-time validated |
   340→| `order` | number | no | Sort order within the group on the index. Default `0` |
   341→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   342→
   343→**File structure determines hierarchy:**
   344→
   345→```
   346→src/content/handbook/
   347→├── culture.mdx                              → /handbook/culture          (top-level)
   348→├── how-we-build-software.mdx               → /handbook/how-we-build-software  (top-level, has children)
   349→└── how-we-build-software/
   350→    ├── front-end-development.mdx           → /handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development
   351→    └── back-end-development.mdx            → /handbook/how-we-build-software/back-end-development
   352→```
   353→
   354→- A page becomes a **parent** when a sibling directory shares its name and contains MDX files.
   355→- Children inherit their parent's URL as a prefix.
   356→- Nesting beyond one level is supported but should be used sparingly.
   357→- The `group` field is still required on child pages — it controls which group they appear under in sidebar navigation.
   358→
   359→## Export
   360→
   361→All collections must be exported from `src/content.config.ts`:
   362→
   363→```ts
   364→export const collections = {
   365→  projects,
   366→  blog,
   367→  labs,
   368→  pages,
   369→  highlights,
   370→  clients,
   371→  testimonials,
   372→  awards,
   373→  "handbook-groups": handbookGroups,
   374→  handbook,
   375→};
   376→```
   377→
   378→## Draft System
   379→
   380→Drafts are managed via the **`.draft` filename suffix**, not a frontmatter field.
   381→
   382→### How It Works
   383→
   384→| File | Dev (`pnpm dev`) | Prod (`pnpm build`) |
   385→|---|---|---|
   386→| `cool-project.mdx` | ✅ Loaded | ✅ Loaded |
   387→| `cool-project.draft.mdx` | ✅ Loaded (preview) | ❌ Excluded at glob level |
   388→
   389→### Workflow
   390→
   391→1. **Create a draft:** name it `my-post.draft.mdx` (or `.draft.md`, `.draft.yaml`)
   392→2. **Preview locally:** run `pnpm dev` — drafts are included in the content layer
   393→3. **Publish:** rename `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`
   394→4. **Unpublish:** rename `my-post.mdx` → `my-post.draft.mdx`
   395→
   396→### Important: References to Drafts
   397→
   398→If a non-draft entry (e.g. a highlight or award) references a draft entry:
   399→- **In dev:** works fine — the draft is in the collection
   400→- **In prod build:** fails with a build error — the draft is excluded, so the reference is broken
   401→
   402→This is intentional. It prevents shipping pages that link to unpublished content.
   403→
   404→## How Relationships Work
   405→
   406→All cross-content references use `reference()` from Astro for compile-time validation. `reference()` returns `{ id, collection }` objects, **not** full entries. You must resolve them with `getEntry()`.
   407→
   408→### Pattern 1: Related Projects in a Case Study
   409→
   410→Frontmatter in `src/content/projects/cool-project.mdx`:
   411→
   412→```yaml
   413→---
   414→title: Cool Project
   415→client: Acme Corp
   416→date: 2024-06-01
   417→thumbnail: /images/projects/cool-project/thumb.jpg
   418→relatedProjects:
   419→  - another-project
   420→  - third-project
   421→---
   422→```
   423→
   424→If `another-project` or `third-project` don't exist as files in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails immediately**.
   425→
   426→In the page template `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`:
   427→
   428→```astro
   429→---
   430→import { getCollection, getEntry, render } from "astro:content";
   431→import { mdxComponents } from "../../components/mdx/components";
   432→
   433→export async function getStaticPaths() {
   434→  const projects = await getCollection("projects");
   435→  return projects.map((project) => ({
   436→    params: { slug: project.id },
   437→    props: { project },
   438→  }));
   439→}
   440→
   441→const { project } = Astro.props;
   442→const { Content } = await render(project);
   443→
   444→// Resolve related projects
   445→const relatedProjects = project.data.relatedProjects?.length
   446→  ? await Promise.all(project.data.relatedProjects.map((ref) => getEntry(ref)))
   447→  : [];
   448→---
   449→
   450→<Content components={mdxComponents} />
   451→```
   452→
   453→### Pattern 2: Homepage Highlights (Cross-Collection)
   454→
   455→Each highlight references an entry in another collection via `discriminatedUnion`. Resolved in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→export async function getResolvedHighlights() {
   459→  const allHighlights = await getCollection("highlights");
   460→  const sorted = allHighlights.sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
   461→
   462→  const resolved = await Promise.all(
   463→    sorted.map(async (h) => {
   464→      const entry = await getEntry(
   465→        h.data.source.collection,
   466→        h.data.source.entry.id,
   467→      );
   468→      return {
   469→        label: h.data.label,
   470→        collection: h.data.source.collection,
   471→        entry,
   472→      };
   473→    }),
   474→  );
   475→
   476→  return resolved;
   477→}
   478→```
   479→
   480→### Pattern 3: Awards with Project References
   481→
   482→Awards reference projects, resolved in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
   483→
   484→```ts
   485→export async function getAwards() {
   486→  const allAwards = await getCollection("awards");
   487→  const sorted = allAwards.sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
   488→
   489→  const resolved = await Promise.all(
   490→    sorted.map(async (a) => {
   491→      const project = await getEntry("projects", a.data.project.id);
   492→      return {
   493→        award: a.data.award,
   494→        year: a.data.year,
   495→        url: a.data.url,
   496→        project,
   497→      };
   498→    }),
   499→  );
   500→
   501→  return resolved;
   502→}
   503→```
   504→
   505→### Pattern 4: MDX Cross-Sell Component
   506→
   507→Inside any `.mdx` file:
   508→
   509→```mdx
   510→<ProjectCrossSell slug="another-project" />
   511→```
   512→
   513→The component resolves the slug with `getEntry()` and throws at build time if not found.
   514→
   515→## Collection Helper Functions
   516→
   517→All query helpers live in `src/lib/collections.ts`. These handle sorting and reference resolution. **No draft filtering is needed** — drafts are already excluded by the content loader in production.
   518→
   519→| Function | Returns |
   520→|---|---|
   521→| `getProjects()` | All projects, sorted by date desc |
   522→| `getPosts()` | All blog posts, sorted by date desc |
   523→| `getLabs()` | All labs, sorted by date desc |
   524→| `getClients()` | All clients, sorted by order asc |
   525→| `getTestimonials()` | All testimonials, sorted by order asc |
   526→| `getResolvedHighlights()` | Highlights with referenced entries resolved |
   527→| `getAwards()` | Awards with referenced projects resolved |
   528→| `getHandbookGroups()` | All handbook groups, sorted by order asc |
   529→| `getHandbookPages()` | All handbook pages, sorted by order asc |
   530→| `getTopLevelHandbookPages()` | Handbook pages without a parent |
   531→| `getHandbookChildren(parentId)` | Direct children of a handbook page |
   532→| `getHandbookParent(entry)` | Parent entry for a handbook page, or null |
   533→| `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry)` | Ancestor chain from root to the current page |
   534→| `getHandbookIndex()` | Full grouped structure for the index page |
   535→
   536→## Important Notes
   537→
   538→- **`reference()` returns `{ id, collection }` objects**, not the full entry. Resolve with `getEntry(ref)`.
   539→- **Handbook parent-child hierarchy is file-path derived.** A page at `how-we-build-software/front-end.mdx` is automatically a child of `how-we-build-software.mdx`. No `parent` field in frontmatter — the relationship is implicit in the directory structure.
   540→- **Handbook groups use `reference("handbook-groups")`**, so a typo in `group:` breaks the build, not production.
   541→- **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** No frontmatter field, no query-time filtering. The content loader handles it automatically.
   542→- **Blog supports both `.md` and `.mdx`.** Blog posts that don't need custom components can use plain `.md`.
   543→- **YAML for structured data without prose.** Highlights, clients, testimonials, awards.
   544→- **Config file location:** `src/content.config.ts` (at root of `src/`, both this path and `src/content/content.config.ts` work in Astro v5+, but we use the root path).
   545→- **MDX components are centralized:** registered once in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`, used by all slug pages.

1→# Rules & Anti-Patterns 2→ 3→## DO 4→ 5→- **Use Astro components by default.** Only reach for React when you need client-side interactivity (e.g. mobile menu toggle, form validation). Even then, prefer `client:visible` over `client:load`. 6→- **Use semantic HTML.** `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, ``. Not everything is a `
`. 7→- **Fail at build time.** If a slug reference is broken, a required field is missing, or data is invalid: throw. The build breaks, not production. 8→- **Keep pages thin.** Pages (`src/pages/`) should fetch data and delegate rendering to layout/components. No complex logic in pages. 9→- **Use TypeScript everywhere.** Type your props with `interface Props`, type your data, type your utils. No `any`. 10→- **Use `reference()` for cross-collection links.** Build-time validated. Broken slugs break the build, not production. 11→- **Use `pnpm`.** Not npm, not yarn. 12→- **Use design tokens for colors and typography.** Colors and font sizes come from `tokens.css` custom properties. No hex colors, no arbitrary font sizes. Spacing uses raw values (4px grid guideline, `rem` for vertical, `px` for horizontal when appropriate). See `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`. 13→- **Use `MediaImage` for all images in content.** Never raw `` tags. It constructs CDN URLs with responsive `srcset` and optimization parameters automatically. 14→- **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. In production builds, `.draft` files are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer. In development (`pnpm dev`), drafts are included so authors can preview them locally. Publishing is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`. No frontmatter field needed. 15→- **Use kebab-case for all filenames.** Components, pages, utilities, styles, content — everything is kebab-case (`media-image.astro`, `content-errors.ts`, `global.css`). Enforced by ESLint via `eslint-plugin-check-file`. 16→ 17→## DO NOT 18→ 19→- **Do not use `getStaticPaths` for non-dynamic routes.** Only `[slug].astro` files need it. Static pages like `index.astro` call `getCollection()` directly. 20→- **Do not add a CSS framework.** Scoped `

file.content:
# Rules & Anti-Patterns ## DO - **Use Astro components by default.** Only reach for React when you need client-side interactivity (e.g. mobile menu toggle, form validation). Even then, prefer `client:visible` over `client:load`. - **Use semantic HTML.** `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, ``. Not everything is a `
`. - **Fail at build time.** If a slug reference is broken, a required field is missing, or data is invalid: throw. The build breaks, not production. - **Keep pages thin.** Pages (`src/pages/`) should fetch data and delegate rendering to layout/components. No complex logic in pages. - **Use TypeScript everywhere.** Type your props with `interface Props`, type your data, type your utils. No `any`. - **Use `reference()` for cross-collection links.** Build-time validated. Broken slugs break the build, not production. - **Use `pnpm`.** Not npm, not yarn. - **Use design tokens for colors and typography.** Colors and font sizes come from `tokens.css` custom properties. No hex colors, no arbitrary font sizes. Spacing uses raw values (4px grid guideline, `rem` for vertical, `px` for horizontal when appropriate). See `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`. - **Use `MediaImage` for all images in content.** Never raw `` tags. It constructs CDN URLs with responsive `srcset` and optimization parameters automatically. - **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. In production builds, `.draft` files are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer. In development (`pnpm dev`), drafts are included so authors can preview them locally. Publishing is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`. No frontmatter field needed. - **Use kebab-case for all filenames.** Components, pages, utilities, styles, content — everything is kebab-case (`media-image.astro`, `content-errors.ts`, `global.css`). Enforced by ESLint via `eslint-plugin-check-file`. ## DO NOT - **Do not use `getStaticPaths` for non-dynamic routes.** Only `[slug].astro` files need it. Static pages like `index.astro` call `getCollection()` directly. - **Do not add a CSS framework.** Scoped `

content.content: 1→# Significa Website: Team Discussion & Exploration 2→ 3→> Senior team review of the Significa website rebuild plan. 4→> 4 agents, 4 perspectives, 1 synthesis. 5→> Date: 2026-02-09 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Participants 10→ 11→| Role | Focus | Experience | 12→|---|---|---| 13→| **Technical Architect** | Architecture, build, deploy, performance | 15+ years | 14→| **UX/UI Designer** | Storytelling, components, IA, interactions | 12+ years | 15→| **Marketing/Content Director** | Lead gen, SEO, workflow, content strategy | 15+ years | 16→| **Devil's Advocate** | Stress-testing every decision | 15+ years | 17→ 18→--- 19→ 20→## Consensus: The Plan Is Solid 21→ 22→All 4 participants agree: the technical foundation is well-designed. Astro, static output, Content Collections with Zod, MDX. No one recommended changing the framework or fundamental approach. 23→ 24→Verdict: **ship it, but fix these gaps first.** 25→ 26→--- 27→ 28→## Team Decisions (Post-Discussion) 29→ 30→After reviewing all 4 perspectives, the following decisions were made: 31→ 32→1. **Astro v6 (latest stable).** We go with the latest stable version to avoid near-future breaking changes. No pinning to v5. 33→2. **Media in `public/` for now.** External CDN/image service (ImageKit, Cloudinary, or similar with S3) will be evaluated later. Not blocking for development. 34→3. **No CMS. Marketing uses git + AI.** The marketing team will have the project set up locally. MDX is learnable. AI assistance fills any gaps. Good documentation and content templates are key. 35→4. **Components: start lean, grow with content.** The 5 starting components are sufficient. New components are added when content demands them, not eagerly pre-built. 36→5. **Conversion architecture evolves with data.** CTAs and conversion points will be designed and tested against real metrics. Marketing owns this. 37→6. **SEO infra from inception.** RSS, sitemap, canonical URLs, robots.txt, 404 page, and SEO component should be set up from the start. 38→7. **CSS token system: no magic values.** Tokens evolve with the design but the rule is absolute: no hardcoded pixels, no hex colors, no arbitrary spacing. Agents must follow this strictly. 39→8. **Slug collision check: included.** Maximize compile-time validations. `reference()` over plain slugs, always. Non-technical content editors need the build to catch their mistakes. 40→9. **`reference()` is non-negotiable.** The Astro lock-in is worth the build-time validation. Runtime errors are unacceptable for a site managed by non-developers. 41→ 42→--- 43→ 44→## 5 Critical Gaps (Unanimous or Near-Unanimous) 45→ 46→### 1. Media Pipeline Is Underspecified 47→ 48→**Raised by:** All 4 participants. 49→ 50→The plan says "CDN handles it" but doesn't define the actual pipeline. Cloudflare R2 is object storage, not an image CDN. 51→ 52→**What's missing:** 53→- URL patterns for responsive images (`srcset`, format=webp/avif) 54→- Upload workflow (Figma to CDN to MDX) 55→- `width` and `height` as required props on `MediaImage` (prevents CLS) 56→- `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"` for hero images (LCP) 57→- Cloudflare Image Resizing or Workers for transformation 58→- Local development experience with CDN images 59→ 60→**Tech Architect's solution:** 61→ 62→```astro 63→--- 64→// MediaImage with responsive images via Cloudflare Image Resizing 65→interface Props { 66→ src: string; 67→ alt: string; 68→ width: number; 69→ height: number; 70→ eager?: boolean; 71→ sizes?: string; 72→} 73→ 74→const { src, alt, width, height, eager = false, sizes = '100vw' } = Astro.props; 75→const cdnBase = 'https://media.significa.co'; 76→const transforms = (w: number) => 77→ `${cdnBase}/cdn-cgi/image/width=${w},format=auto,quality=80/${src}`; 78→--- 79→ 80→ 81→ 85→ 94→ 95→``` 96→ 97→**Recommendation:** Define the image pipeline before writing any content. Build a CLI tool or script that uploads to R2 and generates the MDX snippet. 98→ 99→**Team Decision (Updated):** Media is managed via the internal asset manager app (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads to S3. A self-hosted image transform service on Fly.io serves media through `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare edge cache → Fly.io → S3) with sharp-based image transforms (resize, quality, format conversion via URL query parameters). See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` for the full technical reference. 100→ 101→--- 102→ 103→### 2. Content Workflow Without CMS: Realistic But Limited 104→ 105→**Raised by:** Marketing, Devil's Advocate, Tech Architect. 106→ 107→Git + MDX works if 1-2 technical people control the content. The breaking point comes when non-technical team members need to publish. 108→ 109→**Devil's Advocate scenario:** 110→> Tuesday afternoon. A marketing manager wants to fix a typo. They need to: open the repo, find the .mdx file, edit without breaking YAML, commit to a branch, open a PR, wait for build, get it merged, wait for deployment. In a CMS: click, edit, save. 30 seconds. 111→ 112→**When it works:** 113→- 1-2 people write content AND are comfortable with git 114→- Publishing cadence is low (2-4 posts/month) 115→- No external contributors 116→ 117→**When it breaks:** 118→- Non-technical team members need to contribute 119→- Publishing frequency increases 120→- Blog posts sit in PRs because the dev who merges is busy 121→ 122→**Recommendation:** Don't add CMS now, but: 123→1. Create MDX skeleton templates for each content type (copy-paste-edit) 124→2. Document "create new blog post" in 5 steps 125→3. Assign one content owner (not "the team") 126→4. Document Decap CMS as the Phase 2 escape hatch (git-backed, zero lock-in) 127→ 128→**Marketing Director's phased approach:** 129→1. **Phase 1 (now):** File-based MDX. Ship the site. 130→2. **Phase 2 (6-12 months):** Decap CMS or Tina CMS (git-backed visual editor) 131→3. **Phase 3 (if needed):** Headless CMS (Sanity, Contentful, Payload) 132→ 133→**Team Decision:** No CMS. The marketing team will have the project set up on their computers with AI assistance. Changing markdown files is not rocket science. Even with some learning curve, the team should be sufficiently familiar within a week or two. AI fills any remaining gaps, given correct skills and documentation in-project. 134→ 135→--- 136→ 137→### 3. MDX Components Insufficient for Case Studies 138→ 139→**Raised by:** UX/UI (strongly), Marketing, Devil's Advocate. 140→ 141→5 components produce linear, monotonous case studies. The UX/UI Designer was unequivocal: the gap between "portfolio site" and "design studio experience" is in the component toolkit. 142→ 143→**Current 5 components:** 144→ 145→| Component | Verdict | 146→|---|---| 147→| MediaImage | Essential. Needs `size` variant and `eager` prop. | 148→| MediaVideo | Essential. Needs autoplay-on-scroll for showreels. | 149→| ComparisonBlock | Good. Limited use but high impact. | 150→| Metrics | Important. Needs careful design. | 151→| ProjectCrossSell | Necessary. Should support blog/labs too. | 152→ 153→**What a case study looks like with 5 components:** 154→``` 155→Title (markdown) 156→Intro paragraph (markdown) 157→ 158→More text (markdown) 159→ 160→ 161→ 162→Results paragraph (markdown) 163→ 164→``` 165→This is a **linear, single-column scroll**. No rhythm, no visual chapters, no surprise. 166→ 167→**What a compelling case study needs:** 168→``` 169→[Hero: full-bleed image/video, title overlay] 170→[Brief: 2-3 sentences, large type] 171→[Challenge with client quote] 172→[Process: timeline/steps] 173→[Design exploration: image grid] 174→[Key decision: callout block] 175→[Solution: full-bleed dark section with device frames] 176→[Details: comparison, color palette, type specimen] 177→[Results: metrics with context] 178→[Impact: client testimonial] 179→[Next: related project cross-sell] 180→``` 181→ 182→**Components to add (by priority):** 183→ 184→| Component | Justification | Priority | 185→|---|---|---| 186→| `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have | 187→| `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have | 188→| `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have | 189→| `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have | 190→| `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have | 191→| `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have | 192→| `ProcessTimeline` | Visualize design process | Should-have | 193→| `ContentCrossSell` | Generic version of ProjectCrossSell (blog/labs too) | Should-have | 194→| `ColorPalette` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | 195→| `TypeSpecimen` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | 196→| `Embed` | Figma prototypes, CodeSandbox | Nice-to-have | 197→ 198→**Devil's Advocate concern:** Every new component requires a code change and deployment. Document the component addition process so it's a 15-minute task, not a research project. 199→ 200→**UX/UI insight:** Consider using `.md` for blog posts (no custom components needed) and `.mdx` only for case studies. Saves MDX compilation overhead on content that doesn't need it. 201→ 202→**Team Decision:** The 5 starting components are a starting point. New components will be created as content needs arise. We won't eagerly build components that might not be needed. Start simple and lean, grow with the content. 203→ 204→--- 205→ 206→### 4. Conversion Architecture Is Nonexistent 207→ 208→**Raised by:** Marketing (strongly), UX/UI. 209→ 210→The plan has zero conversion points. file.content: # Significa Website: Team Discussion & Exploration > Senior team review of the Significa website rebuild plan. > 4 agents, 4 perspectives, 1 synthesis. > Date: 2026-02-09 --- ## Participants | Role | Focus | Experience | |---|---|---| | **Technical Architect** | Architecture, build, deploy, performance | 15+ years | | **UX/UI Designer** | Storytelling, components, IA, interactions | 12+ years | | **Marketing/Content Director** | Lead gen, SEO, workflow, content strategy | 15+ years | | **Devil's Advocate** | Stress-testing every decision | 15+ years | --- ## Consensus: The Plan Is Solid All 4 participants agree: the technical foundation is well-designed. Astro, static output, Content Collections with Zod, MDX. No one recommended changing the framework or fundamental approach. Verdict: **ship it, but fix these gaps first.** --- ## Team Decisions (Post-Discussion) After reviewing all 4 perspectives, the following decisions were made: 1. **Astro v6 (latest stable).** We go with the latest stable version to avoid near-future breaking changes. No pinning to v5. 2. **Media in `public/` for now.** External CDN/image service (ImageKit, Cloudinary, or similar with S3) will be evaluated later. Not blocking for development. 3. **No CMS. Marketing uses git + AI.** The marketing team will have the project set up locally. MDX is learnable. AI assistance fills any gaps. Good documentation and content templates are key. 4. **Components: start lean, grow with content.** The 5 starting components are sufficient. New components are added when content demands them, not eagerly pre-built. 5. **Conversion architecture evolves with data.** CTAs and conversion points will be designed and tested against real metrics. Marketing owns this. 6. **SEO infra from inception.** RSS, sitemap, canonical URLs, robots.txt, 404 page, and SEO component should be set up from the start. 7. **CSS token system: no magic values.** Tokens evolve with the design but the rule is absolute: no hardcoded pixels, no hex colors, no arbitrary spacing. Agents must follow this strictly. 8. **Slug collision check: included.** Maximize compile-time validations. `reference()` over plain slugs, always. Non-technical content editors need the build to catch their mistakes. 9. **`reference()` is non-negotiable.** The Astro lock-in is worth the build-time validation. Runtime errors are unacceptable for a site managed by non-developers. --- ## 5 Critical Gaps (Unanimous or Near-Unanimous) ### 1. Media Pipeline Is Underspecified **Raised by:** All 4 participants. The plan says "CDN handles it" but doesn't define the actual pipeline. Cloudflare R2 is object storage, not an image CDN. **What's missing:** - URL patterns for responsive images (`srcset`, format=webp/avif) - Upload workflow (Figma to CDN to MDX) - `width` and `height` as required props on `MediaImage` (prevents CLS) - `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"` for hero images (LCP) - Cloudflare Image Resizing or Workers for transformation - Local development experience with CDN images **Tech Architect's solution:** ```astro --- // MediaImage with responsive images via Cloudflare Image Resizing interface Props { src: string; alt: string; width: number; height: number; eager?: boolean; sizes?: string; } const { src, alt, width, height, eager = false, sizes = '100vw' } = Astro.props; const cdnBase = 'https://media.significa.co'; const transforms = (w: number) => `${cdnBase}/cdn-cgi/image/width=${w},format=auto,quality=80/${src}`; --- ``` **Recommendation:** Define the image pipeline before writing any content. Build a CLI tool or script that uploads to R2 and generates the MDX snippet. **Team Decision (Updated):** Media is managed via the internal asset manager app (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads to S3. A self-hosted image transform service on Fly.io serves media through `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare edge cache → Fly.io → S3) with sharp-based image transforms (resize, quality, format conversion via URL query parameters). See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` for the full technical reference. --- ### 2. Content Workflow Without CMS: Realistic But Limited **Raised by:** Marketing, Devil's Advocate, Tech Architect. Git + MDX works if 1-2 technical people control the content. The breaking point comes when non-technical team members need to publish. **Devil's Advocate scenario:** > Tuesday afternoon. A marketing manager wants to fix a typo. They need to: open the repo, find the .mdx file, edit without breaking YAML, commit to a branch, open a PR, wait for build, get it merged, wait for deployment. In a CMS: click, edit, save. 30 seconds. **When it works:** - 1-2 people write content AND are comfortable with git - Publishing cadence is low (2-4 posts/month) - No external contributors **When it breaks:** - Non-technical team members need to contribute - Publishing frequency increases - Blog posts sit in PRs because the dev who merges is busy **Recommendation:** Don't add CMS now, but: 1. Create MDX skeleton templates for each content type (copy-paste-edit) 2. Document "create new blog post" in 5 steps 3. Assign one content owner (not "the team") 4. Document Decap CMS as the Phase 2 escape hatch (git-backed, zero lock-in) **Marketing Director's phased approach:** 1. **Phase 1 (now):** File-based MDX. Ship the site. 2. **Phase 2 (6-12 months):** Decap CMS or Tina CMS (git-backed visual editor) 3. **Phase 3 (if needed):** Headless CMS (Sanity, Contentful, Payload) **Team Decision:** No CMS. The marketing team will have the project set up on their computers with AI assistance. Changing markdown files is not rocket science. Even with some learning curve, the team should be sufficiently familiar within a week or two. AI fills any remaining gaps, given correct skills and documentation in-project. --- ### 3. MDX Components Insufficient for Case Studies **Raised by:** UX/UI (strongly), Marketing, Devil's Advocate. 5 components produce linear, monotonous case studies. The UX/UI Designer was unequivocal: the gap between "portfolio site" and "design studio experience" is in the component toolkit. **Current 5 components:** | Component | Verdict | |---|---| | MediaImage | Essential. Needs `size` variant and `eager` prop. | | MediaVideo | Essential. Needs autoplay-on-scroll for showreels. | | ComparisonBlock | Good. Limited use but high impact. | | Metrics | Important. Needs careful design. | | ProjectCrossSell | Necessary. Should support blog/labs too. | **What a case study looks like with 5 components:** ``` Title (markdown) Intro paragraph (markdown) More text (markdown) Results paragraph (markdown) ``` This is a **linear, single-column scroll**. No rhythm, no visual chapters, no surprise. **What a compelling case study needs:** ``` [Hero: full-bleed image/video, title overlay] [Brief: 2-3 sentences, large type] [Challenge with client quote] [Process: timeline/steps] [Design exploration: image grid] [Key decision: callout block] [Solution: full-bleed dark section with device frames] [Details: comparison, color palette, type specimen] [Results: metrics with context] [Impact: client testimonial] [Next: related project cross-sell] ``` **Components to add (by priority):** | Component | Justification | Priority | |---|---|---| | `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have | | `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have | | `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have | | `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have | | `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have | | `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have | | `ProcessTimeline` | Visualize design process | Should-have | | `ContentCrossSell` | Generic version of ProjectCrossSell (blog/labs too) | Should-have | | `ColorPalette` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | | `TypeSpecimen` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | | `Embed` | Figma prototypes, CodeSandbox | Nice-to-have | **Devil's Advocate concern:** Every new component requires a code change and deployment. Document the component addition process so it's a 15-minute task, not a research project. **UX/UI insight:** Consider using `.md` for blog posts (no custom components needed) and `.mdx` only for case studies. Saves MDX compilation overhead on content that doesn't need it. **Team Decision:** The 5 starting components are a starting point. New components will be created as content needs arise. We won't eagerly build components that might not be needed. Start simple and lean, grow with the content. --- ### 4. Conversion Architecture Is Nonexistent **Raised by:** Marketing (strongly), UX/UI. The plan has zero conversion points. After reading a brilliant case study, the visitor encounters nothing saying "let's work together." **What's missing:** - `CTA` component embeddable in MDX (headline, body, button, link) - Contact form (Formspree, Netlify Forms, or similar) - Newsletter signup (Buttondown, Resend) - Contextual CTAs at the end of case studies and blog posts - Strategic CTA placement on homepage **Marketing Director's math:** > 5,000 monthly visitors at 1% contact rate = 50 leads. With proper conversion architecture (CTAs, newsletter, social proof) = 2-3% = 100-150 leads. For a studio, that's predictable pipeline vs. relying on referrals. **Recommended CTA placements:** - **Case studies:** "Want results like these? Let's talk." at the bottom - **Blog posts:** Newsletter signup mid-article and at bottom - **Labs:** "Built by Significa. See our client work." crosslink - **Homepage:** Primary CTA above the fold, secondary after work section **Team Decision:** The final structure will have conversion points, but this is something to try and test against real data and metrics. Not a worry for the initial build. Marketing people own this and will iterate based on performance. --- ### 5. SEO and Technical Infrastructure Missing **Raised by:** Marketing, Tech Architect. Elements required for launch day: | Item | Status | Priority | Effort | |---|---|---|---| | RSS Feed (`@astrojs/rss`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | Sitemap (`@astrojs/sitemap`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | 404 page | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | Canonical URLs | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | Robots.txt | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | `` component (OG, Twitter, meta) | Partial | Must-have | Medium | | JSON-LD structured data | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | | Tag archive pages | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | | View Transitions | Mentioned, not planned | Should-have | Medium | | Pagefind (search) | Not mentioned | Nice-to-have | Low | **Tech Architect's SEO component:** ```astro --- interface Props { title: string; description: string; ogImage?: string; type?: 'website' | 'article'; publishedAt?: Date; } const { title, description, ogImage = 'https://media.significa.co/og-default.jpg', type = 'website', publishedAt } = Astro.props; const canonicalURL = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site); --- {title} {type === 'article' && publishedAt && ( )} ``` --- ## Secondary Gaps (Important but Not Blockers) ### Content Types Missing **Team/People collection** (raised by UX/UI, Marketing): - Name, role, photo, bio, social links - Feeds into: author cards on blog, project credits, about page - A design studio sells people. Without this, the studio feels faceless. **Testimonials** as structured data (raised by Marketing, UX/UI): - Client name, role, company, quote, associated project - Embeddable in case studies AND aggregatable on homepage - Single highest-ROI missing content type per Marketing Director **Careers collection** (raised by Marketing): - Title, department, location, type, description, requirements - Enables Google Jobs structured data - Static "careers page" is insufficient if attracting talent is a goal ### Analytics (Raised by Marketing, Tech) Nothing mentioned in the plan. Unanimous recommendation: - **Plausible or Fathom** (privacy-first, no GDPR cookie banner needed) - **Google Search Console** (free, non-negotiable for SEO) - Track: page views, traffic sources, conversions, content performance ### Version Pinning: Astro v5 Now, v6 When Stable **Team Decision:** Use latest stable Astro. Currently on **v5** — Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs). Upgrade to v6 as soon as it reaches stable. Until then, keep dependencies up to date within the v5 range. ### CSS Token System (Raised by Devil's Advocate, UX/UI) **Team Decision:** Confirmed needed. The token system will be documented and developed alongside the design. No magic numbers and no magic colors must be enforced by all agents. The system grows with the project but the rule is absolute from day one. ### Slug Collision Check (Raised by Tech Architect) **Team Decision:** Include this. We should include as many compile-time validations as possible. A website managed by non-tech and marketing people should error as soon as possible instead of relying on runtime errors. ```typescript const reserved = ['blog', 'projects', 'labs', 'index']; for (const page of pages) { const topSegment = page.id.split('/')[0]; if (reserved.includes(topSegment)) { throw new Error(`Page "${page.id}" collides with reserved route /${topSegment}.`); } } ``` This philosophy extends to all content validation: use `reference()` instead of plain slugs, require fields that must exist, throw on missing data. Build-time errors are features, not annoyances. --- ## Plan Strengths (Full Consensus) What all 4 participants praised: 1. **Build-time validation** with `reference()` and Zod: broken references fail the build, not production 2. **Zero JS by default**: performance is excellent out of the box 3. **Highlights with discriminatedUnion**: elegant, extensible homepage curation 4. **Documentation quality**: clear, opinionated, well-organized (rare for a project plan) 5. **MDX components as Astro (not React)**: correct decision, zero client JS overhead 6. **Media on CDN, not in git**: repo stays lean, deploys stay fast 7. **Content Collections as the "database"**: typed, validated, portable content --- ## Devil's Advocate: Report Card | Aspect | Grade | Note | |---|---|---| | Framework choice (Astro) | B+ | Good fit, but version uncertainty is real | | Content strategy (MDX in git) | B- | Works for dev teams, risky for mixed teams | | Schema design | A- | Well-structured, good references and validation | | Component architecture | B | Solid start, needs growth strategy | | CSS approach | B- | Works at small scale, needs token system | | Media strategy | C+ | Too vague. "CDN handles it" isn't a plan | | Build/deploy | B | Fine for now, needs monitoring | | Documentation | A- | Clear, opinionated, well-organized | | Migration risk | B | Content is portable, glue code is not | | Team scalability | C+ | Works for devs, walls off everyone else | --- ## Interactions and Islands (UX/UI Perspective) ### Where Islands Are Required 1. **Mobile navigation**: `client:load` (immediate) 2. **ComparisonBlock slider**: `client:visible` (in viewport) 3. **Project filtering**: `client:load` (tag-based, immediate results) 4. **Image lightbox/zoom**: `client:visible` (portfolio detail viewing) 5. **Contact form**: `client:load` (validation + submission) 6. **Video player controls**: `client:visible` (CDN-hosted showreels) ### Where Islands Are NOT Needed - Scroll animations: CSS `@scroll-timeline` or Intersection Observer via `

# Significa Website Significa's company website. Static site built with Astro v5, MDX for rich content, TypeScript strict. No CMS, no database, no server. Content lives in git as typed MDX/YAML files. Marketing team manages content directly with AI assistance. **Live site:** significa.co **Stack:** Astro v5 (latest stable), vanilla CSS, pnpm, TypeScript strict, static output to Cloudflare Pages **Path alias:** `@/*` maps to `./src/*` No Tailwind. No CSS framework. No component library. No animation library. --- ## Commands ```bash pnpm dev # Development server with HMR pnpm build # Build static site to dist/ pnpm preview # Preview built site locally pnpm check # TypeScript + content validation (astro check) pnpm lint # Full lint pipeline: Prettier + type check + ESLint pnpm lint:check # ESLint only pnpm lint:fix # ESLint auto-fix pnpm format # Prettier auto-format src/ pnpm format:check # Prettier check (no write) ``` Before pushing: `pnpm lint && pnpm build` --- ## Critical Rules These always apply, regardless of the task. 1. **Static site. No exceptions.** No API routes, no server-side logic, no `fetch()` for content. Everything renders at build time. 2. **Astro components first.** React only for interactive islands with explicit `client:*` directives. If it can be done without JS, do it without JS. 3. **Content Collections are the database.** Use `getCollection()` and `getEntry()` from `astro:content`. Never `import.meta.glob`. Never raw file reads. 4. **References use `reference()`.** Cross-collection links must use Astro's `reference()` for build-time validation. Broken slugs break the build, not production. 5. **Fail at build time. Always.** The site is managed by marketing and non-technical people. Runtime errors are unacceptable. Use `reference()`, require fields, throw on invalid data. 6. **TypeScript strict.** Type all props with `interface Props`. No `any`. 7. **Semantic HTML.** `

`. 8. **No CSS framework.** Scoped `
